# Dax Navarrete

> Computer Science undergraduate at Escuela Politécnica Nacional (Quito, Ecuador) and a
> collaborator on the CMS experiment at CERN. Works on machine learning for particle
> physics — jet flavour tagging and dark matter search analyses — and writes systems
> software in Rust and C. GitHub: @daxrpm.

Also known as: Dax, daxrpm, Ariel Dax Navarrete Freire.
Contact: dax@daxrpm.dev · ariel.dax.navarrette.freire@cern.ch

## Experience

- **CMS Collaborator — CERN, CMS Experiment** (May 2025 – present). Associate contributor working on research reproducibility and software architecture for large-scale scientific computing. Reproduced an antimatter physics paper with the Data Preservation and Open Access (DPOA) group; proposed software architectures for the BRIL group supporting the CERN Phase-2 upgrades; evaluated containerised, Airflow-based research pipelines.
- **Research Assistant — ADA Data Science Laboratory** (Mar 2025 – present). Applied machine learning research on optimization methods, including second-order optimizers. Built Python pipelines for training, evaluation and benchmarking; co-authored a paper on second-order optimization for AI models.
- **AI Developer & Linux Systems Integrator — WORB** (Apr 2025 – Dec 2025). Full-stack development integrating AI into production workflows: backend architecture, AI embedding search and LLM integration, database design, frontend.

## Research (CMS Collaboration, CERN)

- [Dark Matter Search — paper replication](https://github.com/CMS-EPN/search-for-dark-matter-docs): Reproduction of the CMS search for dark matter produced in association with a single top quark or a top quark pair at √s = 13 TeV.
- [MiniParT — jet flavour tagging](https://github.com/CMS-EPN/minipart-topology-vs-flavor): A compact ParticleTransformer-inspired classifier separating H→bb, H→cc and QCD multijet events on CMS Open Data (2016 UL NanoAODSIM). Studies which jet variables carry signal and whether pairwise jet features improve tagging.
- [CMS EPN onboarding guide](https://github.com/CMS-EPN/onboarding-guide): Onboarding documentation for CMS platforms, written for the EPN group.

## Systems software

- [dax-auth](https://github.com/daxrpm/dax-auth): A PAM module written from scratch in Rust that unlocks Linux with face authentication — on-device, encrypted, no cloud. Ships an explicit security model documenting the limits of single-frame liveness on RGB-only hardware.
- [TexTeller-Rust](https://github.com/daxrpm/TexTeller-Rust): Native Rust port of TexTeller's ONNX inference pipeline; converts images of math formulas to LaTeX, byte-exact with the Python reference. Built to embed handwritten-equation recognition into Rnote.
- [io-fs-experiments](https://github.com/daxrpm/io-fs-experiments): C benchmarks comparing O_DIRECT, sendfile() zero-copy, UNIX domain sockets and TCP sockets on ext4.
- [dash](https://github.com/daxrpm/dash): A simple Unix shell written in C.

## AI and applied ML

- [Apogee](https://github.com/daxrpm/apogee): Two-stage rocket ascent simulator using the shooting method to optimize insertion into a circular Low Earth Orbit (160–400 km), with orbital propagation, yaw steering, and 3D trajectory visualization.
- [dax-assistant](https://github.com/daxrpm/dax-assistant): Self-hosted voice-first AI assistant. Hexagonal architecture with one LLMProvider port and four adapters (Ollama, Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini), plus a sandboxed MCP tool layer with confirmation gates.
- [drone-detector](https://github.com/daxrpm/drone-detector): Real-time drone detection with YOLO11 at ~11ms/frame on CPU via OpenVINO, RTSP IP camera support, and WhatsApp alerting.
- [ai-optimizers-benchmark](https://github.com/daxrpm/ai-optimizers-benchmark): Benchmarking suite comparing Adam, AdaMuon and K-FAC optimizers across six workloads.

## Backend

- [epn-tracker](https://github.com/daxrpm/epn-tracker): Degree-progress tracker for EPN students. FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy 2, PostgreSQL, Redis, with academic rules in a pure framework-free domain core.
- [PDFeditor-api](https://github.com/daxrpm/PDFeditor-api): FastAPI service for merging, compressing and splitting PDFs, with ClamAV malware scanning.
